metadata.purpose: Systematic categorization of RAG system failures to guide improvements

retrieval_failures.description: Cases where the correct document chunks are not retrieved
retrieval_failures.impact: No retrieval failures detected - all 75 test cases achieved hit=True

ranking_failures.description: Correct chunks retrieved but not ranked high enough (low MRR)

examples.query: What is the standard return window for products?
examples.impact: User may need to read through irrelevant context

root_causes: Semantic similarity may not capture exact keyword matches
Chunk boundaries may split related information
Multiple similar chunks competing for top positions
mitigation_strategies: Tune chunk size and overlap parameters

incomplete_answers.description: Answers lack completeness or miss important details (correctness < 5)

minor_omissions.description: Correctness score 4/5 - mostly correct but missing minor details

examples.issue: May miss some non-returnable item categories or conditions

examples.query: How long do promotional wallet credits last?
What discount does the referee get on their first order?
What are the shipping options available for orders under ₹999?
What are the warranty options for the Acmera ProBook X15?

root_causes: Retrieved chunks don't contain complete information
Information scattered across multiple chunks not all retrieved
Chunk boundaries cut through cohesive policy sections
LLM fails to synthesize information from multiple chunks
Missing context about edge cases and exceptions
mitigation_strategies: Increase TOP_K to retrieve more context (e.g., 7-10)
Implement parent-child chunking to preserve context
Add metadata to chunks (category, policy type, etc.)
Improve prompt to encourage comprehensive answers
Add specific instructions for handling multi-chunk synthesis

low_faithfulness.description: Answers contain hallucinated or unsupported information (faithfulness < 5)

examples.issue: Minor unsupported details about warranty options
Minor assumptions about business account benefits

root_causes: LLM making reasonable but unsupported inferences
Model filling in gaps with general knowledge
Ambiguous chunk content leading to extrapolation
mitigation_strategies: Strengthen system prompt to emphasize strict grounding
Add explicit instruction: 'Only use information from context'
Add confidence indicators when information is partial
Consider RAG guardrails to detect hallucinations

category_specific_failures.description: Failures concentrated in specific knowledge domains

root_causes: Shipping policy scattered across multiple documents
Complex conditional logic (price tiers, regions, membership)
Frequent policy updates not reflected in chunks
recommended_actions: Add structured metadata for shipping tiers
Increase chunk overlap for shipping sections

root_causes: Return policy has many conditional rules
Exceptions scattered throughout document
Cross-references to other policies (promotions, membership)
recommended_actions: Create FAQ-style chunks for common scenarios

issues: Missing warranty details for specific products
root_causes: Product catalog structure not optimal for RAG
Technical specs may need specialized chunking
Product-specific policies buried in general documents
recommended_actions: Consider separate embedding for product specs

root_causes: Business documentation less comprehensive
Fewer test cases to identify patterns

issues.name: membership_tier_confusion
issues.description: Difficulty distinguishing between Standard, Silver, and Gold membership benefits
issues.mitigation: Add membership tier as chunk metadata, implement tier-aware retrieval

issues.name: policy_exceptions
issues.description: Edge cases and exceptions often missed or incomplete
issues.mitigation: Create dedicated exception chunks, use 'exception' metadata tags

issues.name: numerical_precision
issues.description: Specific numbers (prices, thresholds, limits) sometimes imprecise
issues.mitigation: Extract and store numerical values as structured metadata

issues.name: multi_document_synthesis
issues.description: Answers requiring information from multiple documents less complete
issues.mitigation: Implement graph-based RAG to connect related chunks, increase context window

lowest_scoring_queries_analysis.description: Detailed analysis of the 5 lowest scoring queries (all scored 3/5 on correctness)

failure_type_definitions.retrieval_miss: Hit rate = 0, correct document not retrieved at all
failure_type_definitions.wrong_ranking: Hit rate = 1, correct document retrieved but at position 4 or 5 (low MRR)
failure_type_definitions.hallucination: High hit rate, low faithfulness - making up information
failure_type_definitions.wrong_interpretation: High faithfulness, low correctness - retrieved correct info but misunderstood/incomplete

queries.expected_answer: Opened software, digital downloads, gift cards, perishable goods, and personalized items cannot be returned

failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Retrieved the correct document (MRR=1.0) with perfect faithfulness, but the generated answer likely missed one or more categories of non-returnable items from the comprehensive list (software, digital downloads, gift cards, perishables, personalized items)
failure_analysis.root_cause: List items may be scattered across multiple chunks or the LLM failed to synthesize all enumerated items from the context
failure_analysis.mitigation: Ensure complete lists are kept in single chunks, add explicit prompt instruction to enumerate all items when listing policy exceptions

queries.query: How long do promotional wallet credits last?
queries.expected_answer: Promotional wallet credits have a 90-day expiry from the date of issue and are applied before regular wallet balance during checkout

failure_analysis.primary_issue: Missing details about promotional credit behavior
failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Retrieved correct document with perfect ranking (MRR=1.0) and no hallucination (Faith=5/5), but answer likely stated the 90-day expiry without mentioning the important detail that promotional credits are applied before regular balance
failure_analysis.interpretation_quality: poor - partial answer, missing application order detail
failure_analysis.root_cause: Multi-part question requiring synthesis of two related facts (expiry duration AND usage priority), LLM may have focused only on the direct question about duration
failure_analysis.mitigation: Improve prompt to encourage complete multi-faceted answers, ensure related policy details are co-located in same chunk

queries.query: What discount does the referee get on their first order?
queries.expected_answer: The referee (new customer) receives ₹300 off their first order when they sign up using a referral code and make a purchase of ₹1,000 or more

failure_analysis.primary_issue: Suboptimal ranking combined with incomplete conditional details
failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Correct document retrieved but ranked 2nd (MRR=0.5), and answer likely mentioned ₹300 discount but missed the critical qualifying condition of ₹1,000 minimum purchase requirement
failure_analysis.retrieval_quality: good but not perfect - correct chunk at position 2
failure_analysis.root_cause: Ranking issue meant less relevant context at top position, and the conditional 'if purchase ≥ ₹1,000' clause may have been in a different chunk or overlooked during synthesis
failure_analysis.mitigation: Implement hybrid search to better rank conditional policy statements, ensure discount amounts and their conditions are in same chunk, add prompt instruction to always include qualifying conditions with promotional offers

queries.query: What are the shipping options available for orders under ₹999, and how long does each take?
queries.expected_answer: For orders under ₹999: Standard Shipping (5-7 business days, ₹99) and Express Shipping (2-3 business days, ₹199)

failure_analysis.primary_issue: Incomplete structured information (likely missing costs or durations)
failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Perfect retrieval (MRR=1.0) and no hallucination (Faith=5/5), but answer likely provided shipping methods and durations without including the costs (₹99 for Standard, ₹199 for Express), or vice versa
failure_analysis.root_cause: Shipping policy has structured data (method, duration, cost) that may be formatted as a table or scattered across paragraphs. LLM may have extracted partial attributes for each option
failure_analysis.mitigation: Improve chunking to keep complete shipping tier rows together (method + duration + cost), add table extraction logic, enhance prompt to explicitly request all attributes (options, timing, costs) for shipping queries

queries.query: What are the warranty options for the Acmera ProBook X15, and how does it compare to the Acmera ProBook Air 14 in terms of battery life?
queries.expected_answer: ProBook X15: 1 year standard warranty, extendable to 4 years with Shield Plus. ProBook Air 14: 1 year standard warranty. Battery life comparison details...

failure_analysis.primary_issue: Multi-product comparison with minor hallucination and incompleteness
failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Retrieved correct document (MRR=1.0) but faithfulness dropped to 4/5 indicating minor unsupported details, and correctness at 3/5 suggests incomplete comparison. Likely mentioned warranties but added unsupported details and/or failed to provide complete battery life comparison
failure_analysis.interpretation_quality: poor - two-part question only partially answered
failure_analysis.root_cause: Complex multi-part query requiring information about two different products (warranty options + battery comparison). Product specs may be in separate chunks. LLM attempted to fill gaps with reasonable but unsupported inferences
failure_analysis.mitigation: Improve product catalog chunking to co-locate comparable specs, increase TOP_K for multi-product queries, add explicit grounding instructions to avoid speculation when comparing products, consider structured product metadata/knowledge graph

queries.query: I have a standard membership and I received a defective SoundPods Pro after 18 days. Can I still return it for a refund?
queries.expected_answer: Yes, if the product is defective, the return policy doesn't matter. You can process it as a defective item return and issue a refund as a goodwill gesture

failure_analysis.primary_issue: Defective item exception policy with minor hallucination
failure_analysis.what_went_wrong: Perfect retrieval (MRR=1.0) but faithfulness at 4/5 shows minor hallucination, correctness at 3/5 indicates incomplete exception handling logic. Answer likely stated the return is possible but either made unsupported assumptions about the process or failed to clearly explain that defective items override standard return windows
failure_analysis.interpretation_quality: poor - exception policy logic not fully explained
failure_analysis.root_cause: Support chat logs (15_slack_support_chat.md) contain informal exception handling guidance. The LLM may have tried to formalize the informal guidance and added its own interpretation of 'goodwill gesture' process
failure_analysis.mitigation: Formalize support exception policies in structured format, add metadata tags for 'exception_rules', strengthen prompt to distinguish standard policies vs. exceptions, ensure defective item handling is clearly documented

key_insights: ZERO retrieval misses - all 5 worst queries successfully retrieved correct documents
Primary failure mode: WRONG INTERPRETATION (6/6 cases) - system finds right info but generates incomplete answers
Secondary failure: HALLUCINATION (2/6 cases) - minor unsupported details added when trying to complete partial information
Ranking issue minimal (1/6 cases) - not the main problem
Pattern: Multi-part questions, lists, conditional policies, and product comparisons are most problematic
Faithfulness remains high even in failures (4-5/5) - system rarely makes things up
Root cause: Incomplete information extraction and synthesis, not retrieval failure

summary.generation_needs_improvement: 56% of cases scored below 5/5 on correctness
summary.primary_failure_mode: Wrong Interpretation (incomplete answers) rather than retrieval failures or hallucinations
summary.key_insight: The RAG system excels at retrieval (100% hit rate) and maintaining faithfulness (4.95/5), but struggles with answer completeness, especially for complex multi-conditional policies, lists, and multi-part questions

priority_improvements.justification: 8% of queries have suboptimal ranking (low MRR)
